questions.question_text: What is the primary purpose of an offer in contract law?
options: To invite negotiations
To create a binding agreement
To express willingness to enter a contract
To reject previous offers

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for a valid offer?
options: Clear terms
Communication to the offeree
Acceptance by the offeree
Intent to create legal obligations

questions.question_text: In which case is an offer considered terminated?
options: When the offeree accepts the offer
When the offeror revokes the offer before acceptance
When the offeree makes a counter-offer
When the offeror dies after acceptance

questions.question_text: What distinguishes an offer from an invitation to treat?
options: An offer requires acceptance to form a contract
An invitation to treat can be accepted
An offer is always in writing
An invitation to treat is a binding promise

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of an invitation to treat?
options: A store displaying goods with price tags
A signed contract
A verbal agreement to sell a car
A handshake deal
